title: Asynchronous Collaboration
concept: Designing queues and message inboxes so agents can process peer messages at their own pace.

exercises.prompt: What is a key difference between direct messaging and broadcasting?

options.text: Direct messaging is seen by everyone.
Direct messaging is a private conversation between two parties.
Broadcasting is private between two agents.

exercises.explain: Direct messaging allows two agents to communicate privately, while broadcasting shares information with everyone.

cards.title: What is Asynchronous Collaboration?
cards.body: Asynchronous collaboration lets agents exchange messages without needing to be online at the same time.

cards.title: Definition of Asynchronous Collaboration
cards.body: It refers to communication where agents can send and receive messages at their own convenience.

cards.title: Think of Emails
cards.body: Asynchronous collaboration is like sending emails. You can write and send an email anytime, and the receiver can read and reply when they choose.

cards.title: Example of Asynchronous Collaboration
cards.body: Imagine two AI agents working on a project. Agent A sends a message with updates, and Agent B reads it later when they are ready.

cards.title: Myth: Messages Must Be Read Immediately
cards.body: It's a common myth that messages should be responded to right away. In asynchronous systems, agents have the freedom to respond when they are able.

cards.title: Queues vs. Inboxes
cards.body: Queues and inboxes help manage how messages are delivered and processed.

right.text: Messages that need processing in order.

exercises.prompt: What is an important feature of asynchronous collaboration?

options.text: It requires agents to be active at the same time.
It allows agents to respond at their convenience.
It does not allow message storage.

exercises.explain: The correct answer is that asynchronous collaboration allows agents to respond when they are ready, supporting flexible communication.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In asynchronous collaboration, messages can be sent and received at any time.
exercises.explain: True! Asynchronous collaboration is all about flexibility in sending and receiving messages.

exercises.prompt: Asynchronous collaboration allows agents to respond at their _____ convenience.
exercises.sentence: Asynchronous collaboration allows agents to respond at their own convenience.
exercises.explain: The phrase "own convenience" means agents can choose when to respond.

exercises.prompt: Which statement best describes asynchronous collaboration?

options.text: Agents must be online to communicate.
Agents can communicate without needing to be available at the same time.

exercises.explain: The correct statement highlights the benefit of asynchronous collaboration allowing flexibility in response times.

exercises.prompt: How do queues and message inboxes improve asynchronous collaboration between agents?

options.text: They force agents to respond immediately.
They manage message delivery and allow agents to process messages at their own pace.
They eliminate the need for any messaging.
They ensure all communication is public.

exercises.explain: Queues and inboxes organize messages and let agents work through them when they can, promoting effective asynchronous collaboration.
